issue.title: Fix: Bluesky posts failing ~9.3% of runs due to aggressive filtering and unfetchable sources

successCriteria.description: Domains politico.com, reuters.com, axios.com, thehill.com are filtered out before AI article selection via PAYWALL_DOMAINS
Title keyword similarity threshold raised from 0.5 to 0.7, reducing false-positive duplicate blocks
MIN_KEYWORD_LENGTH raised from 3 to 4, excluding common short words (says, told, over, more) from similarity comparison
SIMILARITY_CHECK_POSTS_LIMIT reduced from 72 to 30, narrowing the comparison window
Image uploads validate Content-Type header before calling upload_blob; invalid MIME types skip image instead of failing the entire post
AI similarity check uses Gemini text/x.enum structured output with SimilarityResult enum, eliminating string parsing
AI article selection uses Gemini JSON structured output with list[SelectedArticle] Pydantic model, eliminating fragile regex parsing
AI tweet generation uses Gemini JSON structured output with TweetResponse Pydantic model, eliminating line-based string parsing
Missing comma bug in domain_lists.py line 51-52 (vaticannews.va + churchofjesuschrist.org concatenation) is fixed
Scrape-blocked domains are distinguished from true paywalls with a clear comment section in domain_lists.py
All existing tests pass after changes
New tests verify MIME type validation: valid image passes, invalid Content-Type skips upload, missing Content-Type skips upload
AI service tests updated to mock response.parsed (Pydantic instances) instead of response.text for structured output methods

assumptions.description: google-genai SDK v1.61.0 (currently installed) supports response_mime_type, response_schema, and response.parsed for structured output
Pydantic is already a transitive dependency of google-genai, so no new dependencies are needed
Field(default=...) is NOT supported in response_schema Pydantic models - SDK raises ValueError
response.parsed returns None when max_output_tokens exceeded or safety filters trigger - must guard with None check
MIN_KEYWORD_LENGTH uses strict > operator (len(w) > setting), so value 4 means words with 5+ characters are kept
timesofisrael.com is already in BLOCKED_DOMAINS (line 92), so it does not need to be added to PAYWALL_DOMAINS
The scrape-blocked domains (politico.com, reuters.com, etc.) may become fetchable if scraping improves, requiring later removal
assumptions.source: Issue #18 Risk section, Pre-Planning Review warning

phases.title: Configuration tuning and domain list fixes
phases.content:
Apply all configuration-only fixes from Issue #18 (Issues 1, 2, 3). These are value changes with no logic modifications.

Tasks:
1. In config/settings.py:
   - Line 96: Change SIMILARITY_CHECK_POSTS_LIMIT from 72 to 30
   - Line 97: Change MIN_KEYWORD_LENGTH from 3 to 4
   - Line 98: Change TITLE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD from 0.5 to 0.7

2. In config/domain_lists.py:
   - Fix missing comma bug on line 51: 'vaticannews.va' is missing a trailing comma, causing string concatenation with 'churchofjesuschrist.org' on line 52
   - Add a new comment section '# Scrape-blocked domains (403/401 errors, not true paywalls)' before the new entries
   - Add politico.com, reuters.com, axios.com, thehill.com to PAYWALL_DOMAINS under this new section
   - Do NOT add timesofisrael.com (already in BLOCKED_DOMAINS)

Note (A5): MIN_KEYWORD_LENGTH uses strict > operator, so value 4 means words 5+ chars are kept. This is consistent with the issue's intent.
Note (A7): Add a comment noting these scrape-blocked domains may need removal if scraping improves.
verification: config/settings.py has SIMILARITY_CHECK_POSTS_LIMIT = 30
config/settings.py has MIN_KEYWORD_LENGTH = 4
config/settings.py has TITLE_SIMILARITY_THRESHOLD = 0.7
config/domain_lists.py has 'vaticannews.va' with proper trailing comma on line 51
config/domain_lists.py has politico.com, reuters.com, axios.com, thehill.com in PAYWALL_DOMAINS
config/domain_lists.py has comment distinguishing scrape-blocked from true paywalls
All existing tests pass: pytest tests/

phases.title: Image MIME type validation before upload
phases.content:
Add Content-Type validation in social_service.py to prevent InvalidMimeType errors from cascading into full post failures.

Tasks:
1. In services/social_service.py lines 177-187, modify the image upload block:
   - After requests.get(), check response.headers.get('Content-Type', '')
   - If Content-Type does not start with 'image/', log a warning and skip the upload (leave thumb as None)
   - If Content-Type is valid, proceed with upload_blob as before
   - This allows the post to proceed without an image rather than failing entirely

2. In tests/test_social_service.py, add tests in TestImageUpload class:
   - test_upload_image_invalid_content_type: Mock response with Content-Type 'text/html', verify upload_blob is NOT called, post still succeeds
   - test_upload_image_missing_content_type: Mock response with empty/missing Content-Type header, verify upload is skipped
   - test_upload_image_valid_content_type: Mock response with Content-Type 'image/jpeg', verify upload_blob IS called

Follow the existing test pattern: patch services.social_service.Client, .requests, .db, .models.

Edge cases:
- Content-Type might include parameters: 'image/jpeg; charset=utf-8' - startswith('image/') handles this
- Content-Type might be missing entirely - default to empty string, skip upload
verification: social_service.py checks Content-Type before calling upload_blob
Invalid Content-Type logs warning and skips upload (thumb stays None)
Post proceeds without image when Content-Type is invalid
3 new tests exist and pass for MIME type validation
All existing tests still pass: pytest tests/test_social_service.py

phases.title: Structured output models and similarity check migration
phases.content:
Define Pydantic models and migrate all 3 AI methods to use Gemini structured output, eliminating fragile regex/string parsing.

Tasks:
1. Add imports at top of ai_service.py:
   - from enum import Enum
   - from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
   - from google.genai import types

2. Define Pydantic models (after FeedPost dataclass, before AIService class):
   ```python
   class SimilarityResult(Enum):
       SIMILAR = "SIMILAR"
       DIFFERENT = "DIFFERENT"

   class SelectedArticle(BaseModel):
       url: str = Field(description="The exact URL from the candidates list")
       title: str = Field(description="The exact title from the candidates list")

   class TweetResponse(BaseModel):
       tweet_text: str = Field(description="Factual social media post under 260 chars")
       hashtag: str = Field(description="One relevant hashtag without # symbol")
       summary: str = Field(description="One sentence summary of the article")
   ```
   IMPORTANT (A3): Do NOT use Field(default=...) - SDK rejects default values.

3. Migrate check_content_similarity (lines 158-171):
   - Create config: types.GenerateContentConfig(response_mime_type='text/x.enum', response_schema=SimilarityResult)
   - Pass config to generate_content
   - Replace response.text.strip().upper() == 'SIMILAR' with response.text.strip() == 'SIMILAR'
   - CRITICAL (A4): Guard with 'if response.text is None: return False' before accessing .text
   - Remove format instruction from prompt ('Return ONLY SIMILAR...' line) - the enum schema handles this

4. Migrate select_news_articles (lines 291-324):
   - Create config: types.GenerateContentConfig(response_mime_type='application/json', response_schema=list[SelectedArticle])
   - Pass config to generate_content
   - CRITICAL (A4): Guard with 'if response.parsed is None' before accessing .parsed
   - Replace regex parsing block (lines 300-324) with:
     parsed_articles = response.parsed
     for article in parsed_articles:
       selected_item = next((item for item in candidate_list if item['URL'] == article.url), None)
       if selected_item:
         selected_articles.append({...})
   - Remove format instructions from prompt ('Return ONLY the URLs and Titles in this format...' section)
   - Keep all selection criteria and MUST AVOID instructions in prompt

5. Migrate generate_tweet (lines 407-416):
   - Create config: types.GenerateContentConfig(response_mime_type='application/json', response_schema=TweetResponse)
   - Pass config to generate_content
   - CRITICAL (A4): Guard with 'if response.parsed is None: return None' before accessing .parsed
   - Replace line-based parsing (lines 414-416) with:
     parsed = response.parsed
     tweet_text = parsed.tweet_text
     generated_hashtag = parsed.hashtag
     summary_text = parsed.summary
   - Remove format instructions from prompt ('Format your response as: TWEET:...' section)
   - Keep all requirements in prompt

**Research Reference:** gemini-structured-output
**From research - Avoid:** Don't duplicate schema in prompt (reduces quality), don't use typing.List (use list), don't use Field(default=...)
verification: SimilarityResult, SelectedArticle, TweetResponse models defined in ai_service.py
check_content_similarity uses text/x.enum with SimilarityResult
select_news_articles uses application/json with list[SelectedArticle]
generate_tweet uses application/json with TweetResponse
All 3 methods guard for None responses (response.parsed is None / response.text is None)
No regex parsing remains in select_news_articles
No line-based string parsing remains in generate_tweet
Format instructions removed from all 3 prompts
No Field(default=...) used in any model

phases.title: Test updates for structured output and final verification
phases.content:
Update existing AI service tests to work with structured output (mock response.parsed instead of response.text) and verify all tests pass.

Tasks:
1. In tests/test_ai_service.py, update TestCandidateSelectionIntegration:
   - Import the new Pydantic models: from services.ai_service import SelectedArticle
   - Update mock_ai_service fixture:
     - Change mock_response.text to also set mock_response.parsed
     - mock_response.parsed should return a list of SelectedArticle instances:
       [SelectedArticle(url='https://example.com/article-1', title='Breaking Article 1'),
        SelectedArticle(url='https://example.com/article-2', title='Article 2')]
     - Keep mock_response.text for backward compatibility if needed

2. Add new integration test for structured output:
   - test_select_articles_structured_output: Verify that when response.parsed returns SelectedArticle list, articles are correctly matched against candidate_list
   - test_select_articles_none_response: Verify fallback when response.parsed is None

3. Add tests for similarity check:
   - test_similarity_check_similar: Mock response.text = 'SIMILAR', verify returns True
   - test_similarity_check_different: Mock response.text = 'DIFFERENT', verify returns False
   - test_similarity_check_none_response: Mock response.text = None, verify returns False (not crash)

4. Add tests for tweet generation:
   - test_generate_tweet_structured: Mock response.parsed as TweetResponse instance, verify correct dict returned
   - test_generate_tweet_none_response: Mock response.parsed = None, verify returns None

5. Run full test suite and verify all tests pass.

**Research Reference:** gemini-structured-output - mock response.parsed instead of response.text
verification: All existing tests in test_ai_service.py updated to work with structured output
New tests for None response handling exist and pass
New tests for structured output parsing exist and pass
Full test suite passes: pytest tests/ -v
No test imports or mocks reference the old regex parsing pattern
